The Story of Climate Catalyst Prize: The Story of Climate Catalyst Prize
The Climate Catalyst Prize was founded o: The Climate Catalyst Prize was founded on a simple belief: the countries facing the greatest climate risks should have the greatest access to solutions, finance, and technical support.
To catalyze climate solutions in LDCs by: To catalyze climate solutions in LDCs by providing the tools, finance, and partnerships needed to transform futures.
We work directly with NGOs, city governm: We work directly with NGOs, city governments, and national ministries to turn climate plans into funded, measurable projects.
Provide technical assistance and capacit: Provide technical assistance and capacity building
Unlock climate funds and carbon revenue: Unlock climate funds and carbon revenue
We focus on Least Developed Countries —: We focus on Least Developed Countries — where climate change hits first and hardest, but where innovation and resilience also run deepest. CCP works as a bridge between LDC governments, local NGOs, municipalities, and global climate finance.
We don't just write reports. We catalyze: We don't just write reports. We catalyze. That means turning plans into funded projects. Turning farms into climate-smart systems. Turning carbon potential into real revenue. Turning adaptation ideas into infrastructure that protects lives.
We help municipalities and communities d: We help municipalities and communities design adaptation plans, early warning systems, and infrastructure that stands up to climate change.
From climate-smart farming to water secu: From climate-smart farming to water security, we support LDC agriculture to become more productive, resilient, and low-emission.
We assist governments and NGOs to build: We assist governments and NGOs to build roadmaps for renewable energy, energy efficiency, and green jobs that drive inclusive growth.
We provide end-to-end support across six: We provide end-to-end support across six core areas to help LDCs build climate resilience and access climate finance.
We help municipalities and communities d: We help municipalities and communities design adaptation plans, early warning systems, and infrastructure that stands up to climate change. Our approach combines local knowledge with global best practices to build lasting resilience.
From climate-smart farming to water secu: From climate-smart farming to water security, we support LDC agriculture to become more productive, resilient, and low-emission. We work with farmers, cooperatives, and government agencies to transform food systems.
We assist governments and NGOs to build: We assist governments and NGOs to build roadmaps for renewable energy, energy efficiency, and green jobs that drive inclusive growth. Our support includes policy alignment, feasibility studies, and implementation planning.
End-to-end support: project identificati: End-to-end support: project identification, methodology selection, validation, MRV setup, and access to voluntary and compliance carbon markets. We help LDCs generate revenue from their climate action.
Project design, feasibility studies, pol: Project design, feasibility studies, policy alignment, monitoring and reporting. We build local capacity to deliver and scale climate projects effectively and sustainably.
We connect organizations in LDCs to GCF,: We connect organizations in LDCs to GCF, GEF, Adaptation Fund, bilateral donors, and private investors. From concept note to full proposal — we guide you through every step.
We measure success by the projects we he: We measure success by the projects we help bring to life and the communities we help protect.
Everything you need to know about the Cl: Everything you need to know about the Climate Catalyst Prize and how we work.
What is the Climate Catalyst Prize?: What is the Climate Catalyst Prize?
CCP is an international NGO dedicated to: CCP is an international NGO dedicated to supporting Least Developed Countries to build climate resilience, transition to low-carbon economies, and access climate finance and carbon markets.
We partner with LDC national governments: We partner with LDC national governments, municipalities, NGOs, community organizations, and development partners to design and deliver climate projects.
We provide 6 core areas: Climate Resilie: We provide 6 core areas: Climate Resilience & Adaptation, Sustainable Agriculture & Water Security, Low Carbon Economy development, Carbon Credits development and trade, Technical Assistance for project design and MRV, and Fundraising and access to climate finance.
Do you only work in LDCs?: Do you only work in LDCs?
Yes. Our mandate is focused exclusively: Yes. Our mandate is focused exclusively on Least Developed Countries as defined by the United Nations. This is where support is needed most.
How does CCP help with carbon credits?: How does CCP help with carbon credits?
We support the full lifecycle: project i: We support the full lifecycle: project identification, methodology selection, validation, MRV setup, and market access. Our goal is to help LDCs generate revenue from their climate action.
How can my organization partner with CCP: How can my organization partner with CCP?
If you are an NGO, government, or commun: If you are an NGO, government, or community group in an LDC working on climate, adaptation, or sustainability, reach out to us. We offer technical assistance and help connect you to funding.
CCP is funded through grants, philanthro: CCP is funded through grants, philanthropic donations, project fees, and partnerships with development agencies and the private sector.
CCP is registered in the USA and operate: CCP is registered in the USA and operates globally with a focus on projects in LDCs across Africa, Asia, Latin America, Caribbean and the Pacific.
If you are an organization in an LDC wor: If you are an organization in an LDC working on climate, resilience, agriculture, or carbon markets — we can help. Let's build projects that deliver adaptation, mitigation, and sustainable development.

Least Developed Countries are identified: Least Developed Countries are identified by the United Nations based on low income, human resource weakness, and economic vulnerability. Our mandate aligns with this official classification.
We support LDCs across Africa, Asia, Lat: We support LDCs across Africa, Asia, Latin America and the Caribbean, and the Pacific. Our portfolio spans diverse contexts from small island states to landlocked nations.
Our engagement is demand-driven. We work: Our engagement is demand-driven. We work where governments, NGOs, and communities request support and demonstrate commitment to climate action.
